_comment: Cross-chain bridge / messaging dependencies detected in Yearn Curation risk reports. Add a bridge and/or a dependency ONLY when a report actually relies on it (a mere mention is not a dependency). Each dependency 'slug' MUST match a file in reports/report/<slug>.md so the row can link to the report and pass scripts/check_bridges.mjs. 'kind' is 'direct' (the assessed asset/protocol itself bridges through it) or 'indirect' (it depends on something else that does). 'model' segments the blast radius of a bridge compromise and MUST be verified, never guessed: 'mint' = the bridge-controlled message path can mint NATIVE token supply, either directly or through a downstream minter (compromise of the required trust path can mint unbacked native supply; account for any quorum); 'lock' = the canonical token is locked/escrowed on the origin chain and the remote is only a bridged representation (compromise is bounded by remote supply + locked collateral); 'transport' = the protocol bridges an UNDERLYING asset (e.g. USDC via CCTP) to remote strategies and the assessed token itself is not bridged, though exposure can persist while assets remain remote; 'unknown' = not yet verified, renders as TODO. 'keywords' drives the reminder scan; 'ignore' lists report slugs that mention the bridge but are NOT dependencies (reviewed, e.g. the report says it does NOT use it). By default a row shows its report's DefiLlama icon; when the report has no usable icon, override with an optional typed 'icon' reference: 'protocol:<slug>', 'stablecoin:<slug>', 'token:<address>', or 'token:<chainId>:<address>'. Bridges are ordered alphabetically by name; keep dependencies sorted direct before indirect. See reports/skill.md and reports/reassessment/SKILL.md for the update flow.

bridges.name: AggLayer (LxLy)
bridges.finality: Ethereum→Katana deposits arrive in ~3–7 min (canonical AggLayer bridge up to ~20 min). Katana→Ethereum withdrawals finalize in about an hour: Katana proves state with OP-Succinct SP1 validity proofs settled on L1 roughly hourly.
bridges.admin: `PolygonZkEVMBridgeV2` is an upgradeable proxy: its implementation is swappable by a ProxyAdmin ([`0x0f99…CC4a`](https://etherscan.io/address/0x0f99738b2FC14D77308337f3e2596b63aE7BCC4a)) owned by a 3-day TimelockController ([`0xEf14…A4EF`](https://etherscan.io/address/0xEf1462451C30Ea7aD8555386226059Fe837CA4EF)), under the Polygon Protocol Council (13 members, 7/13 for standard changes). Because the bridge holds the escrowed L1 funds, a malicious or compromised upgrade could reach them — the timelock is the normal-path warning window, but the Council's emergency route (10/13) can bypass the delay.
bridges.description: The AggLayer LxLy unified bridge (PolygonZkEVMBridgeV2) links Ethereum to AggLayer chains such as Katana. Assets are escrowed in the L1 bridge and a wrapped representation is minted on the destination. Katana additionally uses "Vault Bridge" tokens (e.g. vbUSDC) that deposit the escrowed asset into yield vaults, so a bridge exploit or a fault in that vault can strand or impair the remote backing — holders are exposed to both.

dependencies.name: Yearn yvUSD
dependencies.detail: Katana yvUSDC Compounder wraps USDC into vbUSDC [`0x53E8…a765e`](https://etherscan.io/address/0x53E82ABbb12638F09d9e624578ccB666217a765e) and bridges to Katana

dependencies.name: Flex
dependencies.detail: yvUSD has strategy that bridges funds to Katana. Porotocol doesn't use bridges

dependencies.name: Spectra Finance
dependencies.detail: A live MetaVault on Katana holds Vault Bridge USDC, the MetaVault share itself is not bridged, but its backing sits behind the AggLayer bridge

bridges.name: Chainlink CCIP
bridges.finality: ~15–25 min: waits for source-chain finality, then a DON commit + execute.
bridges.admin: Router and the Risk Management Network are Chainlink-governed; each token pool is owned by the protocol (see per-row owners).
bridges.description: Chainlink's cross-chain interoperability protocol. Two layers: message verification is Chainlink-operated and not protocol-configurable (a committing DON plus an independent Risk Management Network that can halt a lane), comparable to Circle's CCTP attesters. Token movement runs through a token pool the protocol itself deploys and owns — burn/mint or lock/release — where the per-protocol risk lives: the owner sets per-lane rate limits, supported lanes, and who can change them. So the model column reflects the pool mechanism, and the real protocol knobs are rate limits and pool ownership rather than a verifier quorum.

dependencies.name: Apyx apxUSD
dependencies.detail: TokenAdminRegistry maps apxUSD to pool [`0x0e9c…5BB5`](https://etherscan.io/address/0x0e9cA42Bc60bE25F9A67f52173067Cc0Bb405BB5) (LockReleaseTokenPool 1.6.1) — canonical apxUSD is escrowed on Ethereum. Base holds a bridged representation

dependencies.name: Centrifuge JAAA
dependencies.detail: MultiAdapter forwards a message only after matching delivery through both LayerZero V2 and Chainlink CCIP. Compromising either messaging path alone cannot forge a JAAA mint, although MultiAdapter/admin or Spoke vulnerabilities could bypass the quorum.
dependencies.owner: Mint authority: Spoke [`0xEC35…25aB`](https://etherscan.io/address/0xEC3582fcDc34078a4B7a8c75a5a3AE46f48525aB), governed by Centrifuge Root — 48h timelock [`0x7Ed4…368f`](https://etherscan.io/address/0x7Ed48C31f2fdC40d37407cBaBf0870B2b688368f).

dependencies.name: Maple (syrupUSDC)
dependencies.detail: LockReleaseTokenPool holds ~13% of supply on mainnet, backing bridged syrupUSDC on Base/Arbitrum/Solana

bridges.name: Circle CCTP
bridges.finality: ~13–19 min standard (source hard finality); CCTP V2 Fast Transfer ~8–20 s.
bridges.description: Circle's Cross-Chain Transfer Protocol moves native USDC by burning on the source chain and minting on the destination, gated by Circle's attestation service — a 2-of-2 signature quorum whose attesters are both Circle-operated and swappable by Circle. Canonical CCTP removes third-party lock-and-mint bridge risk, but the trust assumption reduces to Circle's availability and attestation finality.

dependencies.name: Gauntlet gtUSDa
dependencies.detail: Bridges USDC across Base/Arbitrum/Optimism to Morpho

dependencies.name: Origin OUSD
dependencies.detail: Cross-chain strategies bridge ~42% of TVL to Base and HyperEVM

dependencies.name: Yearn yvUSD
dependencies.detail: Bridges USDC to Arbitrum and Base; Katana uses AggLayer separately

dependencies.name: Flex
dependencies.detail: yvUSD has strategy that bridges funds to Base. Porotocol doesn't use bridges

bridges.name: LayerZero
bridges.finality: Seconds to ~15 min — set per route by the DVN block-confirmations (see Route Security).
bridges.admin: `EndpointV2` is immutable; each OFT adapter is owned by the protocol (see per-row owners).
bridges.description: Generic cross-chain messaging. Tokens bridge via the OFT / OFT-Adapter standard; security depends on the configured [DVN (Decentralized Verifier Network)](https://docs.layerzero.network/v2/concepts/modular-security/security-stack-dvns) set and the token's mint/burn authority on each chain. The Route Security column shows the DVN quorum for the specific route named below the badge — how many independent verifiers must attest a message before it executes — where a 1-of-1 is a single point of failure (the configuration behind the April 2026 rsETH incident). LayerZero configuration is directional and can differ by route; a badge must not be read as protocol-wide coverage. Values are read onchain from the named route's receive-side ULN config, and the badge links to that receive-side OApp.

dependencies.name: Cap stcUSD
dependencies.detail: Adapter [`0x983a…4137`](https://etherscan.io/address/0x983aeaaa0d0426839158435c43725ea7f45d4137) escrows 25,311,191 stcUSD. Katana runs a native OFT at the same vanity address [`0x8888…8888`](https://explorer.katanarpc.com/address/0x88887bE419578051FF9F4eb6C858A951921D8888)

dependencies.name: InfiniFi
dependencies.detail: siUSD adapter [`0x5f21…c3c0`](https://etherscan.io/address/0x5f2106bb2a5aba6a783dbf29c8d3b09c175bc3c0) escrows siUSD and iUSD adapter [`0xdd1c…3005`](https://etherscan.io/address/0xdd1cb2e1aa483e1d94e3e22e70cfbb634fcb3005) escrows iUSD; both mint native OFTs on Katana. Neither adapter holds RECEIPT_TOKEN_MINTER, so they cannot mint native supply

security.label: 4-of-4
security.route: Ethereum→Katana (siUSD mint side; iUSD verified separately with the same quorum)

dependencies.name: Paxos USDG
dependencies.detail: OFTWrapper is Supply Controller SC3 with 45M USDG mint capacity (~521 USDG/sec refill) — the bridge can mint native USDG
dependencies.owner: Adapter owner: Paxos Operations MPC wallet [`0x3Af3…024B`](https://etherscan.io/address/0x3Af3e85f4f97De7AD0f000B724Fb77fE5ffc024B).

dependencies.name: Re (reUSD)
dependencies.detail: ReMintBurnAdapter holds a live cross-chain mint path on reUSD; active on Avalanche, Arbitrum, Base, BNB; 2.5M/24h rate limit

dependencies.name: Midas mHYPER
dependencies.detail: Adapter holds M_HYPER_MINT_OPERATOR_ROLE (mint has no onchain backing check) and the burn role

dependencies.name: Sky USDS
dependencies.integration: LayerZero V2 OFT Adapter (Solana + Avalanche only)
dependencies.detail: Minor route: LayerZero handles only Solana + Avalanche (~48.7M locked, <0.7% of mainnet USDS supply) and does not hold `wards` on USDS. The bulk of USDS/sUSDS bridges via native rollup bridges — see the Native Rollup Bridges row

dependencies.name: Across Protocol
dependencies.detail: Across's deployed Arbitrum adapter maps Ethereum USDT to the USDT0 OFT messenger for HubPool→SpokePool rebalancing; the assessed V2 LP token is not itself bridged, and other assets use separate transports

security.label: 3-of-3
security.note: USDT0 route only; other Across assets use separate bridge configurations

dependencies.name: Centrifuge JAAA
dependencies.detail: Spoke [`0xEC35…25aB`](https://etherscan.io/address/0xEC3582fcDc34078a4B7a8c75a5a3AE46f48525aB) holds mint authority on JAAA; MultiAdapter requires both LayerZero and CCIP, while the LayerZero leg requires Deutsche Telekom + Canary and 2-of-3 P2P/Nansen/Nethermind

security.label: 4-of-5
security.note: 2 required DVNs plus 2-of-3 optional DVNs; LayerZero is also one leg of a 2-of-2 quorum with CCIP

dependencies.owner: Mint authority: Spoke [`0xEC35…25aB`](https://etherscan.io/address/0xEC3582fcDc34078a4B7a8c75a5a3AE46f48525aB), governed by Centrifuge Root — 48h timelock [`0x7Ed4…368f`](https://etherscan.io/address/0x7Ed48C31f2fdC40d37407cBaBf0870B2b688368f).

dependencies.name: Fluid
dependencies.detail: Inherits rsETH's LayerZero OFT Adapter model

dependencies.name: KernelDAO hgETH
dependencies.detail: rsETH bridges via OFT Adapter [`0x85d4…8Ef3`](https://etherscan.io/address/0x85d456B2DfF1fd8245387C0BfB64Dfb700e98Ef3), which escrows 46,254 rsETH (~9.6% of supply) — it cannot mint native rsETH

security.label: 4-of-4
security.route: rsETH OFT Adapter (Arbitrum↔Ethereum); Kelp docs confirm 4-of-4 on the L2 mint path

bridges.name: Native Rollup Bridges
bridges.type: Canonical L2 bridges (OP Stack / Arbitrum Nitro)
bridges.finality: L1→L2 in minutes; L2→L1 withdrawals ~7 days (optimistic-rollup fraud-proof window).
bridges.admin: Each rollup's bridge is a proxy controlled by that chain's ProxyAdmin / Security Council.
bridges.description: Each rollup's own canonical bridge — the OP Stack StandardBridge (Base, Optimism, Unichain), the Arbitrum Nitro gateway, and equivalents. L1 tokens are escrowed and minted 1:1 on the L2, secured by the rollup's proof system and Ethereum rather than an external validator set or attestation service. That makes it the lowest added third-party trust of any bridge class here — no separate verifier network to trust. The main residual risk is upgradeability: the bridge contracts can be upgraded, so a malicious or faulty upgrade by their admin is the primary path by which escrowed funds could be moved or minted.

dependencies.name: Sky USDS
dependencies.integration: OP Stack + Arbitrum Nitro canonical bridges (SkyLink)
dependencies.detail: Primary USDS/sUSDS cross-chain route: ~$0.45B USDS + ~812M sUSDS escrowed on L1 and minted 1:1 on Base, Optimism, Unichain (OP Stack) and Arbitrum (Nitro). No bridge holds `wards` on USDS. LayerZero carries only Solana + Avalanche (<0.7% of mainnet USDS supply)
